howItWorks.intro: This function composition calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is function composition?
faq.answer: Function composition means plugging one function into another. f(g(x)) means first evaluate g(x), then plug that result into f. It is written as (f ∘ g)(x).

faq.question: Does order matter in composition?
faq.answer: Yes! f(g(x)) is generally different from g(f(x)). Composition is not commutative. Always pay attention to which function is the outer one and which is the inner one.

faq.question: How do I evaluate f(g(x))?
faq.answer: Step 1: Calculate g(x) for your x-value. Step 2: Take that answer and plug it into f. For example, if g(2) = 7 and f(7) = 15, then f(g(2)) = 15.
